trends.name: Iran War Disrupts Global Oil/Strait of Hormuz
trends.summary: Two weeks into the Iran-US conflict, Tehran controls Strait of Hormuz traffic on its own terms with no pressure to exit. Iran's Foreign Minister now restricts passage to non-US/Israeli vessels and mulls yuan-only trade. Global insurers refusing coverage and sky-high premiums threaten 90% of oil/LNG tankers, creating severe supply chain risk and supporting elevated oil prices despite recent stockpile releases.

trends.name: Energy Sector Divergence: Oil Defensive vs EV Collapse
trends.summary: Honda projects first loss since 1957 ($15.7B) due to failed EV strategy, signaling broader auto industry EV transition pain. Simultaneously, energy stocks and defense plays surge on Iran war geopolitics. SHLD positioned as timely defense play while traditional auto and EV momentum face headwinds.

trends.name: Middle East Escalation: Military Supply Constraints
trends.summary: Israel critically low on ballistic missile interceptors three weeks into Iran war. Switzerland closed airspace for US military flights citing neutrality. US Embassy Baghdad orders Americans to evacuate immediately after attacks on US nationals. Military supply constraints and regional destabilization raise duration/cost risks for ongoing conflict.

trends.name: Fed/DOJ Political Pressure and Rate Policy Uncertainty
trends.summary: DOJ moving to appeal judge's block of subpoenas to Fed in Jerome Powell criminal investigation, signaling ongoing political pressure on monetary policy independence. This uncertainty clouds FOMC meeting outcomes and forward guidance on rates during inflation-volatile geopolitical period.

trends.name: Defense & Defense-Adjacent Stock Rally
trends.summary: Geopolitical tensions driving 'harden your portfolio against Iran risk' strategies with 11-stock defense plays gaining traction. SHLD explicitly positioned as timely defense play; BDC sector showing divergence with strength. War-driven thematic rotation into defense contractors and aerospace gaining investor attention.

trends.name: Oil Price Resilience Despite Stockpile Releases
trends.summary: Global stockpile releases—the largest ever—slowly working into oil market with uncertain impact timeline. Insurance crisis and Strait blockade risks offsetting downward pressure. MarketWatch analysis rejects $150/barrel scenario, but structural supply disruptions prevent price collapse even with SPR releases.

trends.name: AI Valuation Debate: Oracle's Growth-Quality Tradeoff
trends.summary: Oracle's debt-ridden AI ambitions create valuation tension between growth potential and financial quality metrics. Market pricing reflects skepticism on ROI of large capex commitments despite AI revenue tailwinds. Broader TMT sector showing downside risk (XLC rating downgrade) as investors reassess valuations in rising-rate/uncertain-macro environment.

trends.name: Residential Real Estate Value Inflection
trends.summary: Mid-America Apartment upgraded to 'Buy' with valuation now attractive after weakness. Apartment sector pricing inflection point emerging as rates stabilize and war risk reprices residential as defensive asset. Counter to earlier tech-driven portfolio flows.

trends.name: Healthcare Cost Crisis: Medicare Advantage & Benefits
trends.summary: Medicare Advantage facing reality check with seniors bearing cost burden, signaling systemic healthcare inflation and policy vulnerability. Social Security withdrawal numbers reflect desperation (not recklessness), indicating retirement adequacy crisis for middle class. Growing political pressure on entitlements and benefits.

trends.name: Hospitality M&A: Fertitta-Caesars Deal + Icahn Positioning
trends.summary: Fertitta in weekend deal talks to acquire Caesars while Carl Icahn waits in wings, signaling hospitality consolidation and activist positioning. Deal dynamics reflect optimism on casino/travel recovery despite geopolitical headwinds and potential recession risk.

trends.name: Emerging Market & Growth Play Rotation: Grab
trends.summary: Grab's revenue flywheel positioned to drive material growth, reflecting investor appetite for high-growth Southeast Asia exposure. Contrasts with domestic-focused defensive rotation but indicates selective risk-taking in high-growth regions less affected by Iran geopolitics.

trends.name: AI Utility Monetization: OpenAI's Future Business Model
trends.summary: OpenAI's Altman positions AI as utility commodity ('intelligence on a meter'), signaling shift toward recurring subscription/usage-based revenue model. Reflects long-term AI industry maturation strategy but raises questions on competitive moats and pricing power as market commoditizes.
